content.p: The President shall establish a SEED Information Center System, using existing Executive branch agencies and acting in cooperation with the Government of Poland and the Government of Hungary.

paragraph.heading: In general
paragraph.chapeau: The SEED Information Center System shall serve as a central clearinghouse mechanism for information relating to—

subparagraph.content: business needs and opportunities in Eastern Europe, and
voluntary assistance to countries in Eastern Europe.

paragraph.heading: Private enterprise development
paragraph.chapeau: The SEED Information Center System shall be organized, among other purposes, to encourage—

subparagraph.content: the submission of economically sound proposals to the Polish-American Enterprise Fund and Hungarian-American Enterprise Fund, and
other sources of finance for the development of private enterprise in Eastern Europe.

content.p: The SEED Information Center System shall be based jointly in Washington, District of Columbia; Warsaw, Poland; and Budapest, Hungary; and should it become appropriate, the capitals of other East European countries.

note.heading: Delegation of Functions
note.p: Functions of President under this section delegated to Secretary of Commerce by section 3 of Ex. Ord. No. 12703, Feb. 20, 1990, 55 F.R. 6351, set out as a note under section 5401 of this title.
